question: How many days of annual leave do I get per year?
claude_answer: As a full-time employee, you are entitled to 25 days of annual leave per year. If you are a part-time employee, your annual leave is calculated on a pro-rata basis. You can carry over up to 5 days of unused leave to the next year, but any carried-over leave must be used within the first quarter (Q1) of the new year or it will be forfeited. You can view your leave balance anytime on the HR self-service portal.

question: How many days can I work from home per week?
claude_answer: You can work remotely up to 3 days per week. The remaining 2 days must be spent in the office unless otherwise agreed with your line manager. You must also be available online and responsive during core hours (10am to 3pm) regardless of where you work. Remote work arrangements must be agreed with your line manager in writing.

question: What is the L&D budget per employee per year?
claude_answer: Every employee is entitled to a personal Learning and Development (L&D) budget of £1,500 per year. This budget can be used for external courses, certifications, conferences, books, and online learning platforms. All L&D spend must be pre-approved by your line manager before booking.

question: Can I carry over 10 days of unused annual leave?
claude_answer: No, you cannot carry over 10 days of unused annual leave. According to the Annual Leave Policy, you can carry over a maximum of 5 days per year. Any carried over leave must be used within the first quarter (Q1) of the new year or it will be forfeited. If you have more than 5 days unused, the excess days beyond 5 will be lost.

question: Can I work from home every day of the week?
claude_answer: No, you cannot work from home every day of the week. According to the Remote Work Policy, employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week maximum. The remaining 2 days must be spent in the office unless otherwise agreed with your line manager in writing. However, if you have specific circumstances, you may discuss alternative arrangements with your line manager to see if an exception can be made.

question: Can I work remotely from another country for 3 months?
claude_answer: No, you cannot work remotely from another country for 3 months. Remote work from outside your country of employment requires prior approval from both your line manager and HR department, and is limited to a maximum of 4 weeks per calendar year for tax and compliance reasons. Your request for 3 months (12 weeks) exceeds this limit.

question: Can I take unpaid leave to travel for 6 months?
claude_answer: Based on the Annual Leave Policy, you are entitled to a maximum of 25 days of paid annual leave per year, with a limit of 15 consecutive working days without prior written approval from your department head. The policy does not explicitly address unpaid leave for extended periods like 6 months. To explore unpaid leave options, you should contact HR directly, as this falls outside the standard annual leave framework and would require special consideration and approval from senior management.
claude_policy_area: Annual Leave Policy / HR Special Requests

question: What happens to my bonus if I resign in February?
claude_answer: According to the Compensation and Bonus Policy, bonus payments are made in March each year for the previous financial year. If you resign in February, you would not be eligible to receive the bonus unless your employment contract contains a specific clause stating otherwise. I recommend reviewing your individual employment contract to see if there are any special provisions that apply to your situation.
